title: expand_vars: return Cow to avoid alloc
description: expand_vars always returns String, forcing a heap allocation on every call. When variables_enabled is false it clones the full string unconditionally. When variables_enabled is true but the input contains no ${...} sequences it still builds a new String char-by-char. Callers (eval_header_test, eval_address_test, eval_envelope_test, eval_exists_test) collect these into Vec<String> on every test evaluation, paying allocation cost even in the common variables-disabled case. Returning Cow<'_, str> and borrowing the input when no substitution is needed would eliminate these allocations on the warm evaluation path.
